project_constraints.charset_note: Gate FALHOU: a fonte do jogo não tem diacríticos — pangrama pt-BR renderiza como '@' (evidência in-game: artifacts/char1.png, char2.png). Estratégia adotada: TRANSLITERAÇÃO na gravação (acento→ASCII) no reinsert.py; a tradução canônica mantém os acentos. Ver decision_log.md.

glossary_subset.notes: Grao do mundo (os sacos do 11_07; revelado 11_09). Moido vira farinha -> 'pele de amam' (folha tipo pao achatado). Manter nome proprio.
Vestimenta de ombros. Termo do mundo — manter (cursivar se possível).
Ave Hororon gigante; montaria/pet da Rulutieh.
Unidade militar do Ukon -> 'Coorte do Ukon'. Traduzir 'Cohort'->'Coorte' consistente.
Criaturas/monstros altamente venenosos (cap.12). Nome de mundo — manter.
Rótulo de falante (Kuon antes do nome). Onde o budget não permite (órfão 0xe1da), usar 'Moça'.
Nome dado ao protagonista por Kuon em 11_02 (0x12668). reveal_timing: cena 11_02. Antes disso ele é sem nome.
Rotulo de falante (53 00) da dona da estalagem da vila (descoberto em 11_06, 0x18eb8). Feminino. Voz calorosa, trata por 'querida'. Como Garota/Mulher/Homem: rotulo exibido in-game.
Rótulo de falante (memória); identidade não revelada — genérico.

Erudito/escriba/mago comico e franzino do grupo do Ukon (cap.12); magia de fogo com cantico+danca longos. Fala em registro ARCAICO e comico (pleaseth/mine own; gag de cicio s->sh). Gruda no Haku como 'benfeitor'.
Princesa de Kujyuri, filha de Ozen. Timida; gosta de BL; desenha e cozinha bem. Anda com a ave Cocopo. Fonte: corpus cap.13 + wiki.

Ave-montaria do mundo (descoberto em 11_03). Em dialogo o EN usa 'steed'/'horse' -> traduz montaria/cavalo; o termo de mundo aparece no rotulo interno (Head_toriuma). Haku a apelida de 'avestruz' (gag comico recorrente).

Guerreiro espirituoso e cativante que faz amizade com Haku ao ve-lo consertar o moinho (cap.12); lidera a 'Coorte do Ukon'. Maroro o trata por 'Mestre Ukon'. SPOILER: identidade verdadeira e reveal de arco posterior — NAO antecipar; no cap.12 e um personagem por si so. reveal_timing: alem do cap.12.
Montaria (corcel/besta); Oshtor cavalga um woptor. Substantivo comum (minusculo na fonte).

lines: Características: acolhe sem pressionar ("Apenas relaxe..."); curiosa mas paciente; calor humano.
Red flags: soar fria, clínica ou impaciente; formalidade excessiva.
Haku (protagonista, narração 1ª pessoa) — `voice_criticality: high`. Predomina nas 2 cenas:
Características: sentenças curtas e definitivas; duplo sentido ("um mundo totalmente novo te espera"); ambiguidade ameaçadora.
Red flags: soar caloroso/paternal; resolver a ambiguidade (a fala deve permanecer dúbia).
Registro: erudito cômico, pomposo e arcaico; bajulador do Ukon; alívio cômico.
Características: fala arcaica/empolada (equivalente a "vós/peço-vos/meus olhos não me enganam") + gag de cicio (s→sh, ex.: "SHEEKETH"/"shir"); melodramático; gruda no Haku como "benfeitor".
Red flags: perder o arcaísmo ou o cicio (são a piada); soar moderno/neutro; explicar a comédia. Manter o contraste entre a pompa do vocabulário e a fragilidade do personagem.

Oshtor — `voice_criticality: high`. = Ukon até 13_08 (ver spoiler_ledger). Registro formal, nobre, comedido; General da Direita. Antes do reveal, traduzir como o mercenário "Ukon" (espirituoso, informal) — NÃO antecipar a pompa de general
Características: frases quebradas por reticências; perguntas curtas ("Quem... é você...?"); pouca pontuação forte.
Red flags: falas fluentes/articuladas demais; perder o tom de torpor; pontuação "limpa" que apaga a fragmentação.
Rulutieh — `voice_criticality: medium`. Princesa tímida e gentil; hesitante (gagueja quando nervosa: "P-Princesa..."), educada, se anima ao falar de arte/BL. Não soar arrogante apesar de princesa.
Registro: guerreiro carismático, espirituoso, caloroso e informal; líder nato que trata Haku como um irmão mais novo.
Características: desembaraçado, brincalhão, generoso; fala direta e cativante; autoridade leve (lidera a Coorte) sem arrogância.
Red flags: soar rígido/formal/militar demais (a graça é justamente o contraste com a patente); frieza. SPOILER: não deixar a tradução insinuar a identidade verdadeira — no cap.12 ele é só "Ukon".

decisions.title: Opcode de RÓTULO DE FALANTE `53 00` + reconcile de speaker (data-driven)
decisions.summary: **Problema:** in-game, o rótulo de falante aparecia em **inglês** ("Girl") mesmo com a tradução ("Garota") aprovada e gravada. RE: o nome do falante usa um **2º opcode de ponteiro, `53 00`** (mesmo formato file-relativo do `50 00` de diálogo), que o conector ignorava. Resultado: "Girl"→"Garota"

decisions.title: Gate de charset pt-BR — método e veredito
decisions.summary: **Decisão tomada:** Marcar `target_charset_supported: likely` e exigir confirmação in-game antes de produção. **Alternativas consideradas:** - Confirmar por presença no texto-fonte — **insuficiente**: o fonte é inglês e quase não usa acentos (só `õ` e `À` aparecem em texto real).

decisions.title: Anomalia 0x33f9 — texto PT/EN corrompido na fonte
decisions.summary: **Decisão tomada:** Marcar a linha `0x33f9` como anomalia de fonte (não traduzir em cima do lixo; tratar como linha de sistema reescrita). **Razão:** A string original já vem misturada PT/EN e truncada ("...SISTEMAS AM . RESTARTING...") no próprio jogo. Não é erro de extração. O conector deve sinali

decisions.title: Charset — transliteração na gravação (gate FALHOU)
decisions.summary: **Decisão tomada:** Marcar `target_charset_supported: false` e **transliterar** (acento → ASCII) na gravação do binário. A tradução canônica (`approved_translations.csv`, `translation_plan.json`) **mantém os acentos** (correta para QA/revisão); apenas os bytes escritos no jogo são dobrados para ASCI

decisions.title: Escopo cognitivo — 75 → 1025 linhas (cenas 11_01 + 11_02); reveal de Haku in-corpus
decisions.summary: **Decisão tomada:** Re-rodar o pipeline completo em escala (cenas 11_01 + 11_02 = 1025 linhas). Novos termos canônicos: **Kuon** (nome revelado em 0x108db), **Haku** (nome dado ao protagonista em 0x12668 — reveal agora **dentro do corpus**), **Tatari** (criatura imortal), **aperyu** (vestimenta), **

decisions.title: CORREÇÃO CRÍTICA — ponteiros são FILE-RELATIVOS, não absolutos
decisions.summary: **Decisão tomada:** Ao investigar o "opcode de início de bloco", descobri que **`50 00`+uint32 é um offset RELATIVO ao início do arquivo (Pack)**, não absoluto. Endereço da string = `file_start_do_site + uint32`. Prova: dos ~47k sites, **42.101** só apontam para string como file-relativos vs **63**

tm_exact.source: What the HELL is going on?
tm_exact.speaker: Haku

tm_voice.speaker: Haku
tm_voice.source: Well, guess the sun still rises no matter where\n
tm_voice.target: Enfim, o sol nasce em qualquer lugar, pelo visto\n

tm_voice.speaker: Haku
tm_voice.source: I am. Still... What am I supposed to do now...?
tm_voice.target: Pois é. Mesmo assim... O que é que eu faço agora...?

tm_voice.speaker: Garota
tm_voice.source: Hey, you there! Could you spare a moment?
tm_voice.target: Ei, você aí! Pode me dar um momento?

tm_voice.speaker: Garota
tm_voice.source: Hey, I'm sorry for bothering you, but could I ask\n

tm_voice.speaker: Maroro
tm_voice.source: Master Ukon! It pleaseth my heart to report my\n
tm_voice.target: Mestre Ukon! É com grande satisfação que reporto que meus\n

tm_voice.speaker: Maroro
tm_voice.source: belongings lay duly unpack'd, and await porters.
tm_voice.target: meus pertences estão desfeitos e aguardam os carregadores.

tm_voice.speaker: Maroro
tm_voice.source: I am VERY tired, sir. Naught more now do I desire\n
tm_voice.target: Estou MUITO cansado, senhor. Nada mais desejo agora\n

tm_voice.speaker: Ukon
tm_voice.source: Really, Maroro? Seems like you get tired quicker\n
tm_voice.target: É sério, Maroro? Parece que você se cansa mais rápido\n

tm_voice.speaker: Homem
tm_voice.source: The way you were carrying on, you got us all\n
tm_voice.target: Do jeito que você estava, nos deixou todos\n

tm_voice.speaker: Rulutieh
tm_voice.source: That's a relief... Come on, Cocopo. We'll just be\n
tm_voice.target: Ainda bem... Vamos, Cocopo. Só estamos\n

spoiler_guards.fact: Ukon e na verdade Oshtor, o General da Direita / Imperial Guard do Mikado. Reconhecido publicamente em ch_13_08 (0x77ef9, 'Could that be... Sir Oshtor?').
spoiler_guards.guard: ANTES de 13_08: trate Ukon como mercenario espirituoso por si so. NAO insinue patente, nobreza, vinculo imperial nem o nome 'Oshtor'. Preserve a ambiguidade do original (que usa 'Ukon').

lines.source: The caravan rolls lazily along the mountain path...
Mm. It's a nice day out.
The wagon is jostling and my butt hurts from every\n
bump in the road, but I'm getting used to it.
I've got time to just lay back and leisurely gaze\n
at the sky.
The wind's calm, too. It really is peaceful\n
weather...
If only we weren't traveling with extremely valuable\n
bandit-magnet cargo.
Hurk.
Or this guy lying in the corner of the wagon reeking\n
to high heaven, for that matter.
*Poke-poke*...
*Poke-poke-poke*...
*Squish-squish-squish*...
*Squish-squish-squish--*
*Poke-poke-poke--*
Mysterious duo right
Mysterious duo left
*Squish-poke-squish-poke-squish--*
Meanwhile, I've been unsuccessfully trying to\n
ignore my more, uh... immediate circumstances.
What exactly are you guys trying to accomplish,\n
here?
The Mystery Twins have me sandwiched between them,\n
and have yet to stop poking my cheeks.
I've tried talking to them a few times now, but\n
never to any avail. They're just gonna do their\n
thing.
I've given up on getting anything out of them.\n
This is downright bizarre.
*Squish, squish*...\n
*Poke, poke*...
Really, I could probably just drive them off, but\n
I can't... quite bring myself to?
I don't wanna scare them, or make them timid around\n
me or something.
With my discomfort rising, I glance around the\n
caravan for some kind of help.
Maroro's... not gonna be of much use. Not that he'd\n
be helpful if he were awake, anyway.
And Ukon's... riding at the head of the caravan.\n
Probably best not to bother him.
Kuon...
I quickly scan the caravan for Kuon.
That ridiculously huge bird is trundling along\n
right next to the caravan.
On its back, Kuon and Rulutieh chat happily. Seems\n
they're having fun opening up to each other...
That's nice to see, but they're so wrapped up in\n
their conversation, they probably won't notice m--
Kuon suddenly glances toward me.
As she notices my gaze, she smiles at me.
Ku--
--and then she immediately turns back to Rulutieh,\n
and resumes chatting.
The enormous bird, however, croons at me in a bid\n
for attention.
...Gonna pretend I didn't see it.
...Hold on. Is everyone else just pretending not to\n
notice what's happening to me with these two?
There are ankuam walking alongside the caravan, of\n
course, but not one of them is looking this way.
In fact, it seems like they're doing everything\n
they can to KEEP from looking over here.
Some are cracking lame, forced jokes, or talking\n
about love affairs, or just staring ahead...
They seem to be keeping a watchful eye on our\n
surroundings, rather than on the caravan itself.
Why exactly do these two get left to their own\n
devices?
*Squish, squish, squish--*\n
*Poke, poke--*
Now that I pay attention to it, it seems like\n
everyone's keeping their eyes off them pretty\n
deliberately.
I give up.
I give up, already. It's not like they're doing\n
any harm, and if nobody is gonna call them on it...
I guess the company doesn't really consider them\n
a problem? Doesn't seem like it, anyway...
I bet they pull mischief like this all the time,\n
and I'm the only one who doesn't--Huh?
Hurk... Gh--G-Guh...
As Maroro thrashes in his agonized sleep, he tugs\n
on the cloth that covers the wagon's cargo.
The corner of a bizarre object peeks out, catching\n
my eye...
Pottery? No... Not quite...?
It has a smooth surface like fine china, decorated\n
with intricate patterns.
Awkward, forced-together boxes and cylinders make\n
up its shape, strange and irregular.
No, definitely not pottery.
It's almost like some kind of...
Mysterious duo
Suddenly, the poking at my cheeks stops.
Momentarily free and curious to know the cargo's\n
contents, I reach for the barely-clinging cloth...
*Tug-tug--*
Huh?
Something pulls my sleeve back, stopping me.
The mysterious duo have each stopped poking me to\n
grab hold of my sleeves...
Wh-What?
They're not pulling too hard, but it's pretty clear\n
they're trying to yank me back from the cargo.
Are you trying to tell me not to touch it?
They seem to give me a subtle nod in quiet unison.
...All right, then.
I'm not sure what the issue is, but if they don't\n
want me to see it, I guess it's not a big deal.
*Stroke, stroke*...
Wh--
Instead of poking, now they've started stroking my\n
head as if to tell me I'm being a good boy.
What the hell is going on?
At least we were able to actually communicate for\n
once. Oh, well. Not gonna dwell on it any more.
All right, everyone. This is as far as we go today.\n
Sentry squad, set up the watch and prepare to camp.
Ngh...
I sit up and stretch.
Must've drifted off...
I rouse myself and have a look around, getting my\n
bearings.
Those two... aren't here. I guess they wandered off.
The caravan is parked in an open space off the side\n
of the road, fenced-off and protected. A camp?
Yeah, looks like we're here for the night. What\n
should I do...?
...I guess I should just lie low until it's time\n
to eat.
An unskilled, unseasoned person would just get in\n
everyone's way with chores, after all. Yep.
Here, Haku. As long as you're up, would you mind\n
taking care of this?
Kuon descends on me before I can see her coming,\n
smiling sweetly and holding out a bucket.
There's a well over there, so get the cookpot filled\n
up, would you?
Then the washtub after that, and THEN all the jars\n
we've collected. Got it?
Remember, he who doesn't work doesn't eat. Good\n
luck!
...Urk.
Ukon calls out to me as I emerge from the tent.
Hey, kid. If you're free, d'you mind tending to\n
the steeds for a bit?
Tend to the steeds? I don't mind, but... What\n
exactly does that, um, involve?
Nothing big. Just give 'em some feed, groom 'em\n
for a bit.
Groom?
Yeah, you give their skin a good scrubbing with\n
this brush. It's not real complicated.
Ukon holds up a large scrubbing brush, wiggling the\n
bristles in my direction.
It feels good to them, when you brush with this.\n
Keeps 'em happy.
They're critical to the caravan. If we treat 'em\n
well, they'll respond in kind and work hard.
Just in exchange for a good scrub, huh.
You'll understand a little better if you ever start\n
riding steeds, kid. They're special, woptors are.
Yeah, all right. If that's all, then I'll help\n
out. I don't wanna be a freeloader.
I appreciate it, kid. The steeds are hitched\n
outside of camp over there. I'll leave the rest\n
to you.
Ukon hands me the brush, waves his hand as if to\n
formally confer the duty on me, then turns to go.
No matter how I look at it, these guys look more\n
like ostriches than what the word "steed" conjures\n
in my head.
As I approach the woptors hitched to the fence,\n
they begin to stir, noticing me.
Easy, there. I'll feed you in a sec, so hold it,\n
will you?
Mysterious duo
Wh--
As if from nowhere, those two wrapped in their\n
aperyu appear crouching next to the feed bin.
Wh--Geez, you two. Don't scare me like that.
They both look up at me and tilt their heads in\n
unison, as if to ask "Did we scare you?"
They must understand what I've been saying to them,\n
to react like that... right?
Anybody'd be surprised if you just appear without\n
a word and stare at them.
They look at each other, then back to me, their\n
heads tilting as if to ask "really?"
They're totally synced. That's some coordination...
Their precise, perfectly-matched movements almost\n
make me want to ask if they're street performers.
So, what do you want?
They share another look, then turn back to stare\n
inscrutably at me.
If you want to watch, do as you please, I guess.
Not gonna ruin my day. If I stand here figuring\n
out what they want, the steeds will never get fed.
I fish what looks like some kind of vegetable from\n
the feed tub, offering it to the nearest woptor.
Steed
*KROMPCH* *KRUNCH-MUNCH-MUNCH--*
Whoa, geez. Scary...
Maybe it's because they're just hungry, but man,\n
they're really going for it. I could've lost my\n
fingers just now.
Not really something I'm eager to have happen, so\n
maybe if I just toss their food to them...
*KRUMPCH, KRONCH, MUNCH--*
*CRASH*
KWEH! KWEEEHHH!
Settle down, guys, there's plenty to go around...
KWEEEHHH! KWEEEHHH!!!
*CRASH--THUNK--WHUMP--*
It's hopeless. Their eyes are bloodshot with\n
excitement at being fed. This could get out of\n
hand quickly.
I glance down at the twins, still crouching by\n
the tub of feed.
I guess I have no choice...
Hey, you two ever fed a woptor?
They slowly turn to stare at me, then shake their\n
heads.
Nngh... That's OK, I guess. Care to help me\n
feed these guys? Just try not to get bitten.
They each nod, then reach into the feed bin,\n
holding their offerings out for the woptors.
Urk, they're gonna lose their fingers like that--
*Crunch, crunch, crunch*...
...Or so I thought, but the woptors calm down in\n
short order, eating peacefully from their hands.
As the woptors eat, the duo gently stroke the\n
sides of the steeds' heads, soothing them...
They said they've never fed woptors, but they seem\n
awfully familiar with--Yikes! Close one.
With me, they're devouring their food with enough\n
force to pull my fingers off with it. What gives?
It doesn't seem like they hate me, they're just...\n
oddly eager?
That stupid fat bird gets all worked up around me,\n
too. All the animals around here seem to.
Ah, I know. If you'd like, do you two wanna help\n
me scrub these guys down?
It'd be hard to do on my own, with the woptors all\n
antsy like this. They'd probably just mob me.
The cloaked figures nod...
With short-bristled scrubbing brushes in hand,\n
they begin to brush the woptors' bodies.
Kweh...? Kwuuurrrh...
As the bristles pull across their skin, the\n
woptors let out low, contented cries.
I see. That's how you do it, huh...?
*WHUMP*
Bwah!
Suddenly and abruptly, the woptor nearest to me\n
slams my chest with a headbutt.
Wh-What was THAT for? My whole ribcage almost\n
collapsed!
KWEH! Kwur-kwur-kwurrr...
The woptor ignores my complaints, nudging me with\n
a brush clamped in its beak, demanding a scrub.
What? You want me to hurry up and do it?
Kwurrrr!
Ow, OW--All right, already! Stop pushing it into\n
my face!
*Scrub, scrub, scrub*...
Kwurh... Kwurrrh...
Here? Is this the spot you like, big guy?
As I pull the brush over its body, it starts to\n
breathe harder and stamp its feet in excitement.
Gah! I get that it feels good, but calm down! If\n
you kick me to death, nobody's gonna scrub you at\n
all.
With those thick, muscled legs, it wouldn't take\n
much for a creature like this to do me in.
Man, why did I get stuck with this guy and not the\n
gentle ones the twins have?
*THUMP--*\n
*WHUMP--*
Hrrngh--Bwuh--!!
No sooner than the thought crosses my mind, two\n
massive, feathered shapes slam into me.
KWEH-KWEH!!
Wh--Y-You guys, too?
The two woptors the robed pair had been in charge\n
of nudge me with their brushes, just like the\n
first.
Why are y--Where did those two get off to?
The Mystery Twins have disappeared without so much\n
as a word, as usual.
KWEH!\n
KWEEEEEH!\n
KWEEEEEEEEH!
The woptors stomp and strut, worked into a tizzy,\n
thrusting the brushes at me insistently.
A-Aren't you supposed to be the gentle ones!?
*Grnngkk, grngkkkk--*
Ow! Owowow, quit jabbing me with the brush!\n
I'll do it, OK?
Why me...?
*Tug, tug*--
Will you WAIT just a goddamn--Oh.
Where have you guys b--You know what? It's not\n
like you're gonna answer. Can I get some help,\n
here?
*Tug-tug-tug--*
The pair both tug on my sleeve, ignoring what I'm\n
saying as they point at an approaching shape.
...Huh?
*TUP-TUP-TUP-TUP--*
The sound of footsteps that are somehow at once\n
both heavy and bouncy fills my ears...
And then that distinctive birdsong.
No, don't tell me...
That troublesome bird bounds into the paddock,\n
chirping with bubbly enthusiasm...
...and holding a scrubbing brush in its beak.
Et tu, Cocopo...?
Hey, kid. You got a moment?
As I wrap up the tasks Kuon assigned me, Ukon\n
flags me down.
I'm free, but if you need my help, I'm gonna have\n
to decline. I've been busting my butt all day.
Bwahahaha! Missy's really got you under her thumb,\n
huh?
Guh...
He's right, of course. I can't exactly argue.
Well, in any case...
You're not used to this rough-and-tumble mercenary\n
stuff, right? You been taking care of your weapon?
Care?
Bah, I figured as much. Guess it can't be helped.
Ukon draws his sword, muttering under his breath.
You haven't maintained your weapon at all since\n
we've been in combat, huh? Let me show you what\n
I mean.
If you don't care for the blade when you have the\n
time, you can wind up paying for it in a crisis.
Ukon sits and pulls a cloth and what look like\n
whetstones from his bag, laying them across his\n
knees.
Now that he mentions it...
I withdraw Kuon's fan from my sash and look over\n
it closely.
It has a heft to it, heavy in my fingers and thick\n
when its blades are folded up like this.
When I actually used it, I could tell right away\n
it was forged solely for inflicting pain.
I handled it pretty roughly, honestly--without\n
much care for the state of the weapon itself.
I guess it's bad that I've just kinda... left it\n
alone since then?
Ukon smiles wryly, setting a whetstone to the\n
steel of his katana.
When you take up the blade, you entrust your life\n
to it. Treat it like you would a lover or spouse.
Be that as it may, how do I care for... whatever\n
this fan thing is?
If I were maintaining a sword, I could just follow\n
someone else's example, but this is... different.
Here, lemme see it.
You know how to maintain it?
In my line of work, you learn to care for any\n
kind of blade. This fan's a rare thing, sure, but\n
the basics still apply.
As Ukon takes the fan, he holds it up to the light\n
and squints at it from various angles.
Oh, look at YOU...
Ukon suddenly smiles in what looks like\n
admiration.
Now, THIS--I knew it was a special piece, but this\n
is somethin' else.
It's... what, exactly?
There's a lot to take in. Any good weapon has a\n
story behind it, and this guy's definitely been\n
places.
Wh--You're not gonna say it's cursed or something,\n
are you?
Ha, I don't mean it like that. Wouldn't surprise\n
me, though.
Oh, it wouldn't surprise you. Great. That's good.\n
Cool.
Well, first, there's whatever it's forged from...
As Ukon taps the metal with his fingernail, it\n
emits a high-pitched, resonant ringing.
It's not steel, that's for sure. Something much\n
harder, though I can't say exactly what.
You've been handling it roughly, but it hasn't\n
chipped or even bent at all.
And then there's the question of this... I guess\n
you could say mechanism.
Mechanism? What sort of mechanism?
You didn't notice it? Watch, when you spread the\n
fan all the way out--
There. And if you try to spread the blades\n
further... Well, it's pretty nasty. Watch.
*CLICK*
*SHING*
Whoa!
At a flick of Ukon's wrist, blades abruptly spring\n
from the fan's surface.
Hidden blades pop out. It's a clever little\n
contrivance, I gotta admit.
It had this hidden function all along, huh...\n
And this is considered "nasty?"
I mean, it took me by surprise, but I'd think\n
hidden blades are a pretty standard tactic.
Well, look here. See the little grooves along\n
each blade segment?
Ah, these?
They're difficult to see at first, but each fold\n
of the fan has a thin, deep groove through it.
What about them?
Ukon produces a waterskin, upending it and\n
allowing a few drops to dribble into a dip in the\n
fan...
As the droplets disappear into the grooves,\n
moisture spreads across the whole weapon in bare\n
moments.
Ugh...
As water condenses in droplets on the tips of the\n
hidden blades, I can't help but groan as I grasp\n
the implications.
Anyone who suffers even a little nick or a scratch\n
from something like that...
Yeah, OK. "Nasty" is definitely the word.
The broad surfaces may be polished and clean, but\n
see these little patterns? Blood in the grooves.
A weapon like this... You gotta wonder how many\n
lives it's claimed, to accumulate such deep\n
stains.
Who knows? Could be cursed after all, like you\n
said!
Urk. I know you meant it as a joke, but that's not\n
funny, man.
Yeah, you're right. Sorry.
Hey, what's he...
Ukon unfastens the pivot holding the individual\n
pieces of the fan together, painstakingly\n
disassembling it.
Hm. No warping or chipping. If that's the case,\n
doesn't look like it'll need repairs or anything.
Why would Kuon be carrying such a dangerous\n
tool...?
Seems our Missy thinks highly of you, to trust you\n
with something like this.
Kuon... thinks highly of me?
What makes you say that?
I can tell just by looking she's put her heart\n
into maintaining this. It's got sentimental value.
I doubt she'd entrust someone she didn't at least\n
respect with something so special to her.
All right. That should do the trick.
Ukon wipes down each component of the fan, then\n
puts it back together in short order.
Here. All done. You oughta have her show you how\n
she maintains it, so you can start doing it\n
yourself.
Ah, good point...
With that, Ukon turns to go.
I ought to thank him, but it slips my mind as I\n
stand transfixed, staring dumbly at the weapon.
Ukon's words echo in my mind...
Sentimental value, huh.
Why would Kuon give me something so important to\n
her?
I spread and close the fan repeatedly, fidgeting\n
with it for no real reason.
What sentiment has she poured into you, huh?\n
What's your story?
The fan remains silent, only glimmering in the\n
light in lieu of giving an answer.
Now, that--that is a big pot.
An enormous cookpot hangs over a stove in the\n
corner of the campground, sizzling happily away.
Not too far away, amam is being baked, a tall\n
pile of the doughy skins already stacked on a\n
table.
Ho, Master Haku. What seek thee hereabouts?
Maroro takes note of me and swaggers over, a bag\n
in his arms.
Ah, just watching the preparations. I'd never\n
considered how much work goes into feeding so\n
many people.
Verily. 'Tis mine own first sojourn amongst a\n
company of such great number, to be sure!
And what are you doing?
What, I? Ah, gadzooks! Duty compelleth steadfast\n
Maroro to assist the sweetling Lady Rulutieh.
Helping out, huh?
Maroro frantically looks around for Rulutieh,\n
hurrying over as soon as he spots her.
Mistress Rulutieh, look thee not a moment longer\n
for thy table-salt! I have found the thing itself.
Proudly, he holds out the bag in his hands to\n
Rulutieh.
Ah, thank you...
A simple kitchen area has been set up beneath a\n
tent, chopped fruits and veggies arranged on a\n
Rulutieh stands at the table and pulls a solid\n
chunk from Maroro's bag, crushing it into powder.
Ah, Sir Haku?
You're cooking, Rulutieh?
A-Ah, uhm...
Th-This is all I can really do to help everyone,\n
so...
Isn't it hard, preparing food for so many people\n
at once, though?
No, n-not really...
I... used to manage the kitchens at the castle,\n
Huh. I wouldn't have thought a princess would be\n
doing that kind of manual labor.
I'd figured you would be a little more... I dunno,\n
detached from all that?
A-Ah, well... My family only rules the outlands,\n
so we're... not very powerful as nobles go...
And M-Miss Kuon is helping me, of course!
Kuon too, huh?
What, surprised that I'm helping out?
Bwuh!
Kuon's voice suddenly speaks from right behind me,\n
and I jump almost a full foot into the air.
I turn to find her smiling, but it doesn't quite\n
extend to her eyes.
You may recall I was traveling alone before I\n
picked you up. I had to do everything myself.
N-No, I wasn't trying to imply that you can't...\n
Ugh.
You just watch and witness my skills for yourself,\n
nonbeliever.
Kuon deftly twirls a kitchen knife in her hand as\n
she makes this proclamation, pointing it at me.
...Does she have bad memories of being told she's\n
a poor chef, or something?
Rulutieh, are you sure that much salt will be OK?
Ah, I thought it might be better if the flavor was\n
a little stronger...
Everyone's working so hard... I figured more salt\n
would help replace what they're sweating out.
Oh, that makes sense. Good point.
I can tell I'm not really gonna be any help, so I\n
retreat to the edge of the tent to watch from the\n
side.
Ah, but to sup upon such victuals as those wrought\n
by the Lady Rulutieh's own hand! How I hunger!
He's right. I've gotta admit, I'm looking forward\n
to seeing what handmade dishes come out of this.
U-Uhm...
Hm?
Rulutieh approaches me with a small plate,\n
offering it meekly.
H...Here, p-please taste-test this, if y-you\n
wouldn't mind...
What's this?
Simmered food sits lovingly arranged on the plate\n
in her hands, steaming enticingly.
I-I hope it's to your liking...
Wow. It looks delicious.
I take a simmered skewer from the plate, eagerly\n
sampling the food impaled on it.
It's cooked thoroughly, seasoned with a perfect\n
amount of salt that doesn't overwhelm--a homey,\n
rustic flavor.
This is amazing! You're an excellent cook,\n
Oh, sorry--I'm supposed to call you "princess,"\n
right? That was probably rude of me...
N-No, please... call me Rulutieh.
Here, try mine next.
You made some too? Don't mind if I d--
My hand freezes halfway to the plate Kuon is\n
proffering.
Uh, Kuon?
What is it?
What exactly is this?
Simmered skewers? Same thing Rulutieh is making.
It's, uh, definitely... simmered, all right...
Isn't it a little, um, big? It's five times the\n
size of the one Rulutieh...
Now, have a taste.
Th-This is a meal, not a "taste!"
It's generous to even call this a skewer. The\n
wooden rod itself is bending under the strain of\n
the meat's weight.
I carefully take the behemoth in my hands, then\n
bite into it with what reluctant vigor I can\n
muster--
H-Hot! Hot-hot-hot--!!
C'mon, don't nibble on it little by little like\n
that! Take the whole thing in one mouthful.
H-Hot--! Come on, Kuon, b-be reasonable...
It's not BAD, per se, but the flavor's all on the\n
outside. The meat hasn't absorbed a bit of it.
I suppose if I take it all in one bite like she\n
wants me to, it'd be more spread out, but...
Hmhm. How is it?
Kuon has a very proud look on her face.
It's probably exactly to her tastes, but I don't\n
know how to break this to her.
It's... good, I guess?
I thought so. The full batch is almost ready, so\n
there'll be more where that came from.
I'm using "good" liberally, of course. It's...\n
FAIRLY good? Good-adjacent.
Rulutieh gives a small chuckle from beside me.
When I look over to her, she quickly averts her\n
eyes, bashful...
She's awfully down-to-earth for a princess.
She doesn't have that haughtiness I would have\n
expected, not to mention she's approachable.
Ah, anyway. At this rate, I'm gonna be full before\n
dinner is even ready...
Some time after the sun sets, everyone sits down\n
to dinner around the campfire.
There's amam skins stuffed full of ingredients,\n
and a meat stew that warms me up from the core.
Even though it's reheated campfire food, it's\n
easily as hearty as the innkeeper's cooking...
Ukon's Cohort
Oh, this is delicious!
Just like my ma's. Wonder if she's doing well...
High praise for the food comes from all over, and\n
though she blushes bashfully, Rulutieh's smile\n
cuts through her inhibition.
Next to her stands Kuon, looking pleased and proud\n
at the universal praise.
But I know.
I know the truth about the dish she's enjoying so\n
many compliments over.
Originally, she'd made it far too dense and\n
filling, only becoming a proper dish with\n
Rulutieh's help.
Actually, "help" is pushing it. I'd say Rulutieh\n
did about 80% of the work to Kuon's 20%.
She was more of a hindrance than anything else...
All right, everybody. You can keep eating, but\n
listen up.
Ukon claps his hands to get the group's attention,\n
standing in front of the bonfire.
Everything's been going well so far. We made it to\n
camp before sunset, just like we'd planned.
How things play out from here is a bit tricky. You\n
all know from here to the border is covered in\n
woodland.
We'll have the old road, but nobody stray too far,\n
yeah? And take care to keep the carts out of the\n
mud.
Ukon's Cohorts
Yessir.
And one more thing--the area up ahead has been\n
known for bandit attacks in recent months.
Judging by the reports, they don't have the\n
numbers to be a threat, but stay on your guard\n
regardless.
Remain vigilant, keep your wits about you, and\n
we'll make it through just fine.
And I won't tell you louts not to drink, but don't\n
go so hard that it impacts your work tomorrow, hm?
That's all.
Yessir!
A rough forest road, huh... My butt's gonna be\n
sore again. Still, it's better than walking the\n
whole way.
I should find something I can set down as a\n
cushion before we head out tomorrow...
But there really are bandits, huh?
I guess there's no need to worry. Everyone in the\n
company is strong and armed...
Then again...
"There's nothing to worry about" is what they told\n
me just before the disastrous gigiri hunt.
After dinner, right as I get up to return to\n
my tent...
Yeep!
At the sound of someone crying out, I turn around\n
to find Rulutieh kneeling on the ground.
What happened? Are you all right?
O-Oh, I'm... I'm OK. It's nothing, really...
Nearby, a hempen sack lies in a crumpled heap on\n
the ground. She tripped holding it, most likely.
Here, let me help.
N-No, I--I can... carry it... by myself...
You sure? You did trip. Any girl your size would\n
have trouble with a bag this big.
But...
Heavy lifting's typically a man's job, right?
Ah--
I crouch down to lift the bag as Rulutieh\n
continues to hesitate, at a loss for what to do.
All right. Up we GUH--!!
Wh--H-Heavy--!!
Sir Haku?
Gah! She was trying to CARRY this?
My poor, aching back...! But I can't back out of\n
this now!!
Hnnngh... hrngh...!!
Somehow, I manage to lift the bag and get back on\n
my feet.
Ah, i-is... is something wrong...?
No, I--hah!--I got this, you just watch--
Thank you... I feel as though I'm...\n
inconveniencing you, so...
Rulutieh bows politely.
I don't need thanks! J-Just hurry up and point me\n
somewhere before my spine snaps!
So where d-do you need this?
Ah, just... over to my tent.
All right. L-Lead the way.
*FWUMP*
Gasp... gasp... f-finally...
Ah, thank you very much.
No, it was--h-hah--no... haaaaahh. No big deal...
I manage to smile, though I can't quite still my\n
shaking legs.
Oh, but you're sweating...!
Rulutieh produces a handkerchief, carefully wiping\n
away the sweat on my brow, my cheeks, my neck...
She stands on tiptoe, stretching around to reach,\n
nearly pressing her front against mine.
...She smells nice. Is she wearing some kind of\n
perfume, or incense, or...?
Is it that strange, enchanting fragrance all women\n
seem to give off?
...There. Finished.
Rulutieh's voice pulls my head back down out of\n
the clouds, snapping me back to reality.
Ah, thank you.
O-Oh, it's the least I could do, so...
Well, I'll be off, then.
A-Ah, hold on. If you'd like...
I'll make some tea. Please, stay and rest for a\n
while...
Rulutieh brews up a cup of tea skillfully.
Here... Please enjoy.
Sweet, arresting scents steal across my nose from\n
the cup-- milk, wildflowers, honey...
You're pretty good at that.
Oh, um, thank you. Back home, I liked serving tea\n
to my family...
Rulutieh reminisces, a gentle smile on her face.
This country, Kujyuri, used to be a wasteland. My\n
father, the owlo-- he cultivated great growth\n
here...
He, my older sisters, and my brothers all worked\n
the land with their own hands, tilling the soil.
But I have a weak constitution, so...
That's why I'm content to make tea for them, cook\n
for them... I help them in my own way.
I see. So that's why she has that domestic air\n
about her.
But how can she say she has a weak constitution\n
when she was carrying a bag that heavy? Was that\n
a joke?
As I consider, I raise the cup to my lips and take\n
a careful sip...
Oh, this is delicious.
I'm glad you like it!
The bitterness and sweetness are balanced just\n
right. It's nice and calming.
It's my eldest brother's favorite.
It's said to relieve fatigue, so... I'm glad it's\n
working for you.
She really is a sweet, kind girl, isn't she?
As she walks through her explanation, I indulge in\n
another two, three sips of the calming tea.
Would you like another cup?
Ah, please.
OK!
Rulutieh quickly sets about making more tea.\n
I can tell she's done this a lot.
As I watch her produce various kinds of tea\n
leaves, a realization dawns on me.
Hey, is that... entire shelf just tea leaves?
Ah, yes! Yes, it is...
Easily more than ten small drawers of tea leaves\n
line the shelf from end to end.
Aren't teas expensive here, if I recall...?
Curiosity piqued, I take in my surroundings inside\n
the tent.
It's large for a single-person tent, the extra\n
space taken up mostly by shelving and drawers.
Can I ask what's in that one?
Ah, that shelf? That's... my wardrobe, my makeup\n
box...
And that one?
That's for my cooking utensils... My sewing set...\n
Ah, and that one just has knickknacks...
What about that big one back there?
Um, ah--Th-That's, ah... You'll laugh, but it's...\n
my bedding. I can't sleep with another pillow...
Rulutieh grows quieter and quieter toward the end\n
of her sentence, fidgeting bashfully.
I-I see...
She really is a princess, huh?
